physicalDetails.detail: Ranger-built and at ease in the saddle, carrying himself like a field officer who would rather stay on the road than go north into chamber politics.

voiceMarkers.detail: Dry, witty, and politically literate; he teases Sylvanas while keeping the administrative reality in view.
Switches easily from banter to command and from private shorthand to formal, composed authority.

beliefs.belief: The Convocation can still be managed if someone patient enough goes north and fights the argument in its own language.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Ride north and keep the Convocation from treating Jaina's breach as mere court business.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: Buy Sylvanas time and preserve border authority by translating field reality into politics.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Alert, dryly resigned, and already bracing for a political fight he knows will be exhausting.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Accepts the split in responsibilities and takes the patrol north while Sylvanas rides south.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Reads political implications instantly and identifies the likely prisoner before Sylvanas has proof.
Commands the patrol cleanly and pivots from field movement to state response without fuss.

howOthersAddressHim.speaker: Farstrider runner
howOthersAddressHim.pattern: Uses my lord in formal report mode.

howHeAddressesOthers.pattern: Uses Sylvanas with private familiarity and dry challenge.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: Stills visibly when the report turns serious, then resumes control through calm, economical motion.

knowledge.detail: Knows which human mages are important enough to stay in Farstrider dossiers and suspects the prisoner is Jaina Proudmoore.
Understands immediately that the Convocation will try to define the breach as political before it defines it as military.

arc.lie: If he keeps spending wit, patience, and favors, Silvermoon can still be persuaded in time.
arc.truth: The institutions he serves may be structurally too slow for what is coming.
arc.currentPosition: Still functioning as the political translator who believes the argument can be fought.
